channel.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India
channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.
channel.copyright: Copyright:(C) Sat, 11 Jul 2026 02:16:52 IST Bennett Coleman & Co. Ltd, http://in.indiatimes.com/policyterms/1554651.cms

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: You are not the most polished guy: Visa officer asks Indian man why his company is sending him to New York on business trip
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Julius Caesar: 'The greatest power is not in defeating an enemy, but in...'
item.description: Julius Caesar employed a strategy of mercy and generosity to convert adversaries into allies, effectively disarming their resolve by understanding their motivations. Similarly, the ancient strategist Sun Tzu emphasized overcoming enemies without physical confrontation. This principle informs contemporary diplomacy and business negotiations, where fostering ongoing peace and collaboration arises from tackling the root causes of discord.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Scientists reveal underwater seagrass meadows could end global malnutrition
item.description: New research highlights seagrass meadows as vital food sources for coastal populations. Fish from these underwater fields offer a superior nutrient combination compared to reef fish. This finding emphasizes the importance of protecting seagrass alongside coral reefs for food security. Local communities heavily rely on these fish for essential nutrients and income. Conservation efforts must integrate human needs with ecosystem protection for sustainable outcomes.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Connor Murphy allegedly injected gold believing it gave him ‘special powers’ before Thailand death
item.description: YouTuber Connor Murphy reportedly believed injecting gold offered special powers. He died after fleeing police and entering a lake in Thailand. Authorities are awaiting autopsy and toxicology results for clarity. Unconfirmed claims suggest he painted his house gold and acted erratically. Investigations into his death and alleged gold injections continue.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Quote of the day by JD Vance: 'Poor people don't wear pajamas. We fall asleep in our underwear or blue jeans'
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Scientists say coldest 'stars' in Milky Way could actually be alien technology harvesting energy
item.description: Recent studies indicate that certain chill entities scattered across our galaxy might actually be artificial constructs created by extraterrestrial life forms. These colossal structures could function as energy collection systems established by intelligent civilizations. Researchers are on the lookout for distinctive thermal emissions and faint visible light from these objects, leveraging the capabilities of the James Webb Space Telescope and Project Hephaistos.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Pak refuses to accept grooming gang leader unless UK hands over political dissidents
item.description: Pakistan demands UK extradition of dissidents for deporting Shabir Ahmed. Ahmed, a grooming gang leader, faces deportation after his British citizenship was revoked. Pakistan states it will not accept him without reciprocal extradition agreements. Islamabad wants UK extradition of figures like Shahzad Akbar and Adil Raja. This creates a stalemate, with the UK unlikely to extradite dissidents.
item.dc:creator: Naomi Canton

item.title: No, I am in Dublin: PwC worker remotely works from India; lies when confronted, accuses boss of bullying
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg wore a $2 million vintage watch from the 1950s that tracks the cycles of the moon
item.description: Mark Zuckerberg, adorned with a vintage Rolex Stelline, articulates the pivotal role of AI in transforming medical research. His Biohub initiative emphasizes combating human illnesses through sophisticated computer models. The Stelline, a rare and coveted collector's watch, is valued in the millions, reflecting Zuckerberg's refined taste in luxury timepieces, paralleling his push for technological progress in health.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Scientists have debunked the long-repeated myth that baby rattlesnakes are more dangerous than adults, finding that adult rattlesnakes usually deliver more venom and cause more serious bites
item.description: A new study challenges the long-held belief that baby rattlesnakes are more dangerous. Researchers found young snakes can control venom expulsion, similar to adults. Adult rattlesnakes carry and inject significantly more venom, causing more severe symptoms. This misconception has led to negative consequences and unwarranted fear among people. The study traced the myth's spread through media reports over decades.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: A pizza oven window shattered in 37 reported incidents, Cuisinart is recalling the entire grill
item.description: Conair has recalled the Cuisinart Propel+ Four Burner 3-in-1 Gas Grill due to a shattering glass window. Thirty-seven incidents of glass shattering were reported in the United States. One fire incident was also reported, posing a laceration risk to consumers. Customers must remove the glass and upload photos to receive a refund. The grill was sold at Lowes and Walmart from December 2024 to May 2026.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Pakistan launches crackdown after Balochistan attacks, claims 75 BLA militants killed
item.description: In a series of military operations in Balochistan province, Pakistan security forces took decisive action, leading to the death of seventy-five militants. This response came in the wake of fatal attacks on police and civilians blamed on the BLA. An earlier clash near Mangi Dam saw nine policemen and fifteen attackers dead. Prime Minister Sharif's visit to Quetta promised justice and financial compensation for the bereaved families.
item.dc:creator: TOI News Desk

item.title: Green Card of Kuwaiti artist revoked for calling America 'enemy'
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 5 things you probably didn’t know about Erling Haaland, the Viking striker who helped Norway make FIFA World Cup history
item.description: Erling Haaland is making headlines as he propels Norway to an unforgettable World Cup quarter-final by scoring seven goals. Born in England but raised in Norway, his unique background sparks conversations. Haaland, an advocate for social justice, has a notable online presence. Football runs in his blood, as his father was a pro player, and he famously dabbled in rap.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Will Smith: "If you're not making someone else's life better, then you're wasting your time."
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'No more beardos': Pentagon steps up US military grooming crackdown
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Qatari royal buys real estate larger than Vatican City for $375 million in Italy, featuring remote-controlled erupting volcano, five swimming pools and a secret cave to hide from drones
item.description: In a remarkable real estate move, Qatar's Sheikh Hamad bin Jassim bin Jaber Al Thani has acquired the iconic Villa Certosa in Sardinia, previously owned by former Italian Prime Minister Silvio Berlusconi. The transaction, valued at around €350 million, secures a stunning property that spans 120 hectares. Plans are underway to convert Villa Certosa into a luxurious resort, enriching the Sheikh's already impressive portfolio of high-end properties.

item.title: Quote of the day by Haruki Murakami: 'And once the storm is over, you won’t remember how you made it through'
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Three held in Greece over deadly bomb attacks targeting ruling party politicians
item.description: Greece's anti-terrorism forces have taken swift action, detaining three individuals believed to be behind a wave of firebomb assaults targeting members of the ruling New Democracy party. Among the attacks was a tragic explosion in Thessaloniki, which claimed the life of a parliamentary candidate's mother. These arrests not only demonstrate the government's resolve to seek justice but also underscore Greece's challenging history with political extremism.
item.dc:creator: TOI News Desk

item.title: Elon Musk claims SpaceX will send thousands of people to the Moon and Mars within the next 10 years
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk
